title: Unauthenticated route can spend the server Pinata credential and forge evidence metadata

evidence.quote:
const fileRes = await fetch("https://api.pinata.cloud/pinning/pinFileToIPFS", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${PINATA_JWT}` },
        body: fileFormData,

reasoning: The public API route accepts arbitrary form fields, performs no authentication, signature check, escrow membership check, CSRF/rate limiting, or ownership validation, then uses the server-side Pinata JWT to pin caller-supplied files and metadata. Any internet client that can reach the route can consume the project's Pinata quota and publish evidence under arbitrary escrowAddress and submitter values.
reproduction: Send a multipart POST to /api/upload-evidence with any allowed file, escrowAddress set to another escrow, and submitter set to an arbitrary wallet address. The handler will pin it using PINATA_JWT without verifying the caller.
recommendation: Require an authenticated session or wallet signature tied to submitter, verify the submitter is authorized for the escrow before pinning, and add rate limiting or abuse controls before invoking Pinata.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided, and there are no assertions for rejected anonymous, unauthorized, or mismatched submitter requests.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests that anonymous requests and requests with a submitter not authorized for escrowAddress are rejected before any Pinata fetch is made.
minimumFixScope: Add authorization and abuse checks in frontend/src/app/api/upload-evidence/route.ts before reading or uploading the file to Pinata.
